entries.definition:
f–ẓ–ẓ thick, tarnished matter taken from a camel’s belly; to
split open; to be rough, to be rude, to be ill-tempered. Of this root,
فَظّ faẓẓ, occurs once in the Qur’an.

فَظّ faẓẓ [quasi-act. part.] uncouth, rude, gruff, harsh, foul-
mouthed (3:159) وَلَوْ كُنْتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ الْقَلْبِ لَانْفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ had you been
foul-mouthed, hard-hearted, they would have dispersed from
about you.
